bead.title: review: block terminal review classes without no-progress accounting
bead.description:
PROBLEM
Pre-close review now prevents close on REQUEST_CHANGES and BLOCK, but terminal review classes are not first-class operator-blocking outcomes. Spec gaps, missing acceptance criteria, too-large-at-depth-cap, unsafe/out-of-scope changes, and exhausted review errors need to park the bead with needs_human metadata and must not consume no-progress budget.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_post_review.go:141-197 handles reviewer errors by appending review-error or review-manual-required events and setting review_malfunction, but it does not classify exhausted review errors into a stable operator-required stop class.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_post_review.go:233-277 records REQUEST_CHANGES/BLOCK and applies the generic review triage decision, but it does not map findings to review_spec_gap, review_missing_acceptance, review_too_large, or review_unsafe_or_out_of_scope classes.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1262-1275 can still apply no-progress cooldown logic after non-success outcomes unless those outcomes are separately suppressed.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1964-1987 has no terminal-review exclusion vocabulary.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a review terminal classification helper for pre-close review outcomes.
- Map review_spec_gap, review_missing_acceptance, review_too_large at depth cap, review_unsafe_or_out_of_scope, and exhausted review_error to blocked needs_human/operator-required metadata.
- Ensure these terminal review classes append structured review/operator events, unclaim or park the bead consistently, and do not increment no-progress/no_changes budget.
- Preserve the existing APPROVE close path and the existing malformed-review bounded retry behavior until the retry cap is exhausted.
- Leave review_fixable_gap repair scheduling to the sibling repair bead.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement repair cycles in this bead.
- Do not change reviewer prompt content except for any minimal schema labels needed by tests.
- Do not change cost-cap behavior.

DEPS
Depends on ddx-89ab3fda for Blocked stop vocabulary and ddx-224ffcd2 for no-progress-ineligible review outcomes.
bead.acceptance:
1. A production review terminal classifier maps review_spec_gap, review_missing_acceptance, review_too_large at depth cap, review_unsafe_or_out_of_scope, and exhausted review_error into blocked needs_human/operator-required outcomes.
2. TestReviewTerminalClassifications_BlockWithoutNoProgress covers all terminal classes and proves no no-progress/no_changes budget is incremented.
3. TestReviewTerminalClassifications_ExhaustedReviewErrorNeedsHuman covers review_error retry exhaustion and the emitted review-manual-required/operator metadata.
4. Existing TestBoundedReviewRetry_NthFailureEmitsManualRequired, TestPreCloseReview_RequestChangesPreventsClose, and TestPreCloseReview_BlockPreventsClose still pass.
5. APPROVE review behavior remains unchanged: TestPreCloseReview_UnanimousApproveCloses still passes.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "TestReviewTerminalClassifications|TestBoundedReviewRetry|TestPreCloseReview" -count=1 passes.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... passes.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

events.body:
{"rationale":"Strong bead overall. Title is imperative and names subsystem+change. Description has PROBLEM, ROOT CAUSE with file:line references, PROPOSED FIX, and NON-SCOPE. Acceptance criteria are numbered, verifiable, include specific TestXxx names, and end with go test + lefthook gates. Labels cover phase, story, area, kind, reliability, adr, spec, and split-from. Parent is set. Deps are explicit and named with rationale in the description body.

Two minor gaps preventing a 9:
1. AC item 6 names a -run filter that covers three test groups but does not explicitly name TestReviewTerminalClassifications_BlockWithoutNoProgress and TestReviewTerminalClassifications_ExhaustedReviewErrorNeedsHuman by their full names in the filter string — the filter regex 'TestReviewTerminalClassifications' implicitly covers them, which is fine, but AC items 2-3 name those functions and AC-6 should echo them explicitly for traceability.
2. The description does not state 'no deps' or confirm dep direction unambiguously for ddx-89ab3fda — it says 'Depends on' in the DEPS section (good) but the deps array lists both deps without ordering notes; a competent agent could infer the order from the text, so this is minor.

No blocking issues. A sub-agent given only this bead body can identify the files to edit (execute_bead_post_review.go, execute_bead_loop.go), the test names to write, and the gate to pass without asking.","score":8,"suggested_fixes":["In AC item 6, expand the -run filter to explicitly include all named test functions: -run 'TestReviewTerminalClassifications_BlockWithoutNoProgress|TestReviewTerminalClassifications_ExhaustedReviewErrorNeedsHuman|TestBoundedReviewRetry|TestPreCloseReview' to match the functions called out in AC 2-5.","Optional: add a one-line DEPS ordering note confirming ddx-89ab3fda must merge before ddx-224ffcd2 (or vice versa) if there is a sequencing constraint, so the executing agent does not need to infer it."],"waivers_applied":[]}
